disaster-recovery-runbook.description: Render the ADR 0100 ordered recovery plan, current DR evidence summary, and table-top review state.
outputs: Structured tier-by-tier recovery plan for the current LV3 VM layout plus a DR readiness summary.

configure-backups.description: Retained legacy external CIFS backup workflow from ADR 0020.
outputs: Legacy CIFS backup configuration path retained only as a documented blocked workflow.

database-dns.description: Publish the current private database.lv3.org DNS record toward the Proxmox host Tailscale proxy while the PostgreSQL HA VIP remains unapplied live.
outputs: database.lv3.org DNS record updated to the private Proxmox Tailscale PostgreSQL proxy.

route-dns-assertion-ledger.description: Reconcile the ADR 0252 route and DNS assertion ledger drift set across the Hetzner zone, including apex publication truth, tailnet-only DNS targets, and stale record retirements.
outputs: The governed apex, mail, database, and vault DNS records are aligned with the live route assertion ledger.
The stale apex IPv6, autoconfig, notify, and www records are retired from the lv3.org zone.

provision-subdomain.description: Converge one catalogued lv3.org hostname through the Hetzner DNS role and, when applicable, refresh the shared NGINX edge publication.
outputs: The selected DNS record is converged through the Hetzner API.
If the FQDN is backed by a repo-managed edge route, the shared NGINX publication and certificate set are refreshed.

capacity-report.description: Render the repository-managed capacity model report, combining committed allocations, planned growth, and optional live metrics from the monitoring VM.
outputs: A text, markdown, json, or Prometheus-format summary of host capacity targets, active commitments, planned guests, and reserved ephemeral headroom.

weekly-capacity-report.description: Generate the weekly Windmill payload used to post the current platform capacity summary to Mattermost.
outputs: A JSON payload containing the destination channel, metrics source, and markdown-formatted weekly capacity report.

k6-smoke.description: Run the governed ADR 0305 k6 smoke probe against the declared latency-SLO services and fail if the smoke budget or latency threshold is exceeded.
outputs: One k6 smoke receipt per targeted service under receipts/k6 together with the raw summary export captured under receipts/k6/raw.

k6-load.description: Run the governed ADR 0305 weekly k6 load probe, record structured receipts, and publish regression and warning notifications when the latest run exceeds the governed thresholds.
outputs: A dated set of k6 load receipts, raw summary export, optional platform.slo.k6_regression NATS publication, and ntfy warning publication when the remaining error budget drops below 20 percent.

k6-soak.description: Run the governed ADR 0305 monthly k6 soak probe so extended-duration latency and error-budget drift are captured as receipts and surfaced through the platform notification paths.
outputs: A dated set of k6 soak receipts, raw summary export, optional regression events, and warning notifications for long-running latency or error-budget degradation.

deploy-and-promote.description: Validate a staged service change, enforce the ADR 0073 promotion gate, and then apply the approved production promotion.

live-apply-group.description: Run one grouped ADR 0079 live-apply wrapper after the fresh-worktree bootstrap preflight has restored any required generated artifacts.

live-apply-service.description: Run one service-scoped ADR 0079 live-apply wrapper after the fresh-worktree bootstrap preflight has restored any required generated artifacts.

live-apply-site.description: Run the full site live-apply wrapper after the fresh-worktree bootstrap preflight has restored any required generated artifacts.
outputs: A full-site live-apply wrapper that repairs required generated artifacts up front, then runs the shared site convergence path.

live-apply-waves.description: Execute one reviewed dependency-wave manifest so each wave acquires its locks up front and runs its playbooks in parallel.

fixture-expiry-reaper.description: Destroy any ADR 0088 ephemeral fixture whose lifetime window has expired and record one reaper-run receipt for the scan.

ephemeral-vm-reaper.description: Sweep the governed ADR 0106 ephemeral VM pool, retag unowned temporary VMs with a one-hour grace period, and destroy expired VMs.

Untagged VMs in the ephemeral VMID pool are retagged with a one-hour grace period and included in the reaper summary receipt.

ephemeral-pool-reconcile.description: Keep the ADR 0186 prewarmed fixture pools topped up, record spillover decisions, and preserve one warm spare per common fixture profile when local capacity allows.
outputs: Any missing warm members are recreated as stopped or lightly initialized fixtures ready for lease handoff.
Local exhaustion is reported as an explicit auxiliary-domain spillover requirement instead of silently borrowing standby capacity.

check-platform-drift.description: Run the ADR 0071 observation loop checks and emit structured findings from the controller command lane.

close-maintenance-window.description: Close one ADR 0080 maintenance window and emit the matching platform.maintenance.closed or platform.maintenance.force_closed event.
